title: Structured contact extraction
scenario: Write a prompt that extracts structured contact information (name, email, phone) from a block of unstructured text and returns it as JSON.
criteria: specifies the exact JSON schema or field names expected
specifies handling for missing fields (e.g. null vs. omit)
gives at least one example of input to output

title: Tone-controlled short story
scenario: Write a prompt asking for a short story (under 300 words) about a robot discovering emotions, written in a specific, named tone.
criteria: names a specific tone or voice (e.g. whimsical, melancholic)
gives a length constraint
specifies point of view or narrative style

title: Function with edge cases
scenario: Write a prompt asking for a function that validates email addresses, ensuring the response covers common edge cases.
criteria: specifies the programming language
names at least two specific edge cases to handle (e.g. missing @, empty string)
specifies what the function should return (boolean, error message, etc.)

title: Executive summary
scenario: Write a prompt to summarize a technical article for a non-technical executive audience.
criteria: target audience named
length or format constraint given
priorities specified (conclusions vs. methodology)

title: Support ticket triage
scenario: Write a prompt that classifies incoming customer support tickets into categories like Billing, Technical, or General, ensuring consistent category assignment.
criteria: lists the exact allowed categories
gives a rule for tickets that could fit multiple categories
specifies the output format (single label vs. list)

title: Step-by-step how-to
scenario: Write a prompt asking for step-by-step instructions for setting up a home Wi-Fi router, suitable for someone with no technical background.
criteria: specifies a numbered or step-based output structure
names the audience's skill level
specifies whether troubleshooting tips should be included

title: Unbiased product comparison
scenario: Write a prompt asking for a comparison between two competing software products, structured to avoid favoring either one.
criteria: names the specific two products to compare
specifies the comparison criteria or dimensions to use
explicitly instructs neutrality or asks for pros/cons of each

title: Multi-step research task
scenario: Write a prompt asking an AI agent to research a topic across multiple steps (search, synthesize, cite sources) and produce a cited summary.
criteria: specifies that sources must be cited
defines what counts as a successful/complete answer
breaks down or implies the multi-step process expected

title: Formal-to-casual tone rewrite
scenario: Write a prompt that rewrites a formal business email into a casual, friendly tone while preserving all factual content.
criteria: specifies which tone to convert to
explicitly requires preserving factual content or meaning
gives a format constraint (e.g. keep it an email, similar length)

title: The vague request
scenario: Write a prompt asking an AI to "make my writing better."
criteria: adds specificity the original scenario lacks (e.g. names what "better" means: clarity, conciseness, tone)
defines what input text will be provided and in what form
specifies what should be preserved vs. changed
